description_en: Covers radar concepts and systems for object detection, ranging and positioning including the required signal processing and propagation effects.
competencies: Explain concepts for object detection, ranging and angular positioning
Describe the operation of primary and secondary radar systems
Apply signal processing methods for radar
Assess physical propagation effects in radar systems

description_en: Course on computer-aided control design and implementation in Matlab/Simulink, covering robust multivariable and real-time controllers.
competencies: Design and implement controllers computationally in Matlab/Simulink
Auto-tune centralized and decentralized PI/PID controllers
Design robust multivariable controllers via H-infinity and mu-synthesis
Synthesize controllers using linear matrix inequalities and real-time implementation

description_en: A course on the control of AC drives covering space-vector modeling, field-oriented control and direct torque control.
competencies: Model AC machines using space-vector representation
Design control loops for synchronous and asynchronous machines
Apply field-oriented control and direct torque control
Evaluate machine types and control methods for applications

description_en: Examines electricity generation from renewable sources and its integration into the overall energy system and smart grids.
competencies: Assess the impact of renewable sources on the energy supply system
Evaluate photovoltaic, wind and hydro power generation
Analyze grid integration of decentralized renewable generators
Incorporate storage and fuel cells into smart grid concepts

description_en: Design, modelling, control and programming of robot drive systems, including dynamic models and experimental robot investigations.
competencies: Design, model and control robot drive systems
Describe spatial robot motion with Denavit-Hartenberg coordinates
Build dynamic models of robotic systems
Program robot controllers and cascade drive control
Conduct experimental investigations on robot systems

description_en: A seminar on the practical application of cognitive intelligent systems including language modeling and realization in engineering systems.
competencies: Apply principles of cognitive intelligence in practical programs
Build models of acoustic and written language as representation models
Implement cognitive systems in engineering applications
Handle meaning assignment and data in cognitive systems

description_en: Two-semester practical project designing, building and testing sensor systems with microcontroller-based data acquisition and evaluation.
competencies: Design, build and test sensor-based systems for given tasks
Acquire and process measurement data with microcontrollers
Select and evaluate sensors by working principle for application domains
Document and present a two-semester practical sensor project

description_en: A two-semester practical project in which students design, build and test sensor systems for selected application domains.
competencies: Design and build sensor systems for a given application
Select sensors based on their functional principles
Acquire and microcontroller-based evaluation of measurement data
Test, document and present a sensor-based solution

description_en: Structure and programming of programmable logic controllers for binary control and drive automation, with hands-on programming exercises.
competencies: Explain tasks and application fields of programmable logic controllers
Design binary control circuits for asynchronous machines
Program PLC-controlled drive systems
Implement machine and plant control with PLCs
Control motion-control systems with stored-program drive control

description_en: Course on power-electronic systems for power supply, covering renewable generation, HVDC, storage and electric-vehicle charging.
competencies: Implement power-electronic circuits in complex power-supply systems
Design power supplies for renewable generation and electric vehicles
Dimension power-electronic systems application-specifically
Apply HVDC, storage and charging-system concepts in electromobility

title_de: Strukturelle und funktionale Analyse von zellulären Netzwerken
description_en: Structural and qualitative modelling and analysis of cellular networks using graph theory, Boolean networks and metabolic network methods.
competencies: Model cellular networks using graph and hypergraph theory
Perform metabolic network analysis with elementary modes and cut sets
Model regulatory and signalling networks with Boolean networks
Compute structural and statistical network measures
Apply software tools for computational analysis of biochemical networks

description_en: A seminar on system integration of power electronics using modeling, simulation and hardware-in-the-loop for renewable energy and electromobility.
competencies: Address system integration questions for power electronics
Quantify interactions between power electronics and the surrounding system
Apply modeling, simulation and hardware-in-the-loop methods
Represent a complete system including power-electronic assemblies and control

description_en: Conveys advanced concepts of technical cognitive intelligent systems and their practical realization in engineering applications.
competencies: Explain principles of cognitive intelligence and their computer transfer
Work with cognitive model architectures
Implement meaning assignment and data handling in assistive systems
Realize cognitive system concepts in practical programs

description_en: Teaches design and implementation of voice user interfaces and dialogue systems using VXML and modern speech assistants.
competencies: Explain concepts and methods of automatic dialogue systems
Represent knowledge in speech grammars
Build voice dialogues in VXML and Amazon Alexa skills
Evaluate strengths and weaknesses of voice user interfaces

description_en: Covers conversion, design and grid integration of wind turbines for electricity generation including aerodynamics and economics.
competencies: Explain the physics and conversion principles of wind energy
Design wind turbines using aerofoil theory
Calculate power curves and part-load behaviour of wind turbines
Assess grid integration and economics of wind power plants

description_en: Course on wind energy conversion, wind turbine design and components, and the technical and economic aspects of grid integration.
competencies: Explain the physics and conversion principles of wind energy
Design wind turbines using airfoil theory and performance curves
Compute partial-load behavior and power characteristics of turbines
Analyze grid integration and economic viability of wind farms
